26.reason: The loop condition should be i < m, not i <= m, because m is the count of terms, and array indices go from 0 to m-1. Using i <= m accesses a1[m] and n1[m] which are uninitialized, leading to garbage values.

Using i <= m accesses a1[m] and n1[m] which are uninitialized (or leftover from previous input), causing incorrect multiplication.
